Quran Knowledge Graph

Autonomous Discovery

Syllogistic Deduction Engine + AutoResearch Optimization

Fully autonomous research pipeline — zero human intervention

The Problem

6,234 Verses
114 Surahs (Chapters)
  • Deep thematic connections hidden across distant chapters
  • Manual analysis is slow, incomplete, and subject to bias
  • Traditional scholarship studies themes in isolation

The Solution

Knowledge Graph

TF-IDF keyword extraction + weighted graph edges connecting 6,234 verses

AutoResearch

Autonomous parameter optimization — 600+ experiments with zero human input

Syllogistic Engine

Treats every verse as an axiom, computes transitive logical deductions

AutoResearch: Graph Optimization

75.47 → 81.74 Composite Score (+8.3%)
600 Experiments Run
38 Improvements Found
2.5× More Edges per Verse
Key Finding: 2.5× more edges per verse (11 → 28) dramatically improves cluster coherence. Denser graphs surface connections that sparse graphs miss entirely.

AutoResearch: Retrieval Optimization

58.99 → 68.44 Retrieval Score (+16%)
233 Experiments Run
"Less is more" — Fewer, higher-quality results beat broad search. Optimal config returns 50 total results with aggressive deduplication (keep_highest).
  • 2-hop graph traversal with score boosting at each hop
  • Seed limit of 10 keeps focus; hop limits prevent noise

Syllogistic Deduction Engine

Verse A (axiom)
+
Verse B (axiom)
→
Novel Conclusion
Transitive Chains

A→B→C: If verse A connects to B and B to C, deduce A→C

Shared-Subject Synthesis

Two verses about the same subject yield combined conclusions

Thematic Bridges

3-hop chains bridging distant surahs via shared keywords

37,000+ Deductions Generated

118K Deductions / Hour
92% Verses Referenced (5,744 of 6,234)
13 Theological Categories
66% High Quality (score > 70)
500+ cross-theme deductions with quality score above 75 — the most theologically meaningful connections.

Meta-Knowledge Graph

13 theme nodes connected by weighted deduction counts

Prophecy
Afterlife
Moral Law
Creation
←
God's Nature
→
Worship
Covenant
Social Law
History
Central Hub: God's Nature connects ALL other themes — confirming tawhid (monotheism) as the Quran's structural organizing principle.
Strongest Edge: God's Nature ↔ Prophecy — 10,695 bridging deductions

Insight 1: Divine Rescue ↔ Prophetic Mission 92.5

Chain: save → implore → darkness → appreciative → light → Moses
[31:32] People in violent waves who implore GOD sincerely but revert once saved
↓
[6:63] People who promise eternal appreciation if saved from darkness
↓
[14:5] Moses sent to lead people from darkness into light
Physical rescue (storms) parallels prophetic rescue (spiritual darkness). Both require genuine appreciation, but people saved physically often fail to maintain promised gratitude. Moses's mission is the solution: permanent guidance rather than temporary rescue.

Insight 2: God's Will ↔ Social Justice 92.5

Chain: omniscient → wise → charity → free → relative → orphan
[76:30] "Whatever you will is in accordance with GOD's will. GOD is Omniscient, Wise."
↓
[9:60] Charity shall go to the poor, needy, new converts, to free slaves...
↓
[2:177] Righteousness includes giving to relatives, orphans, the needy...
God's omniscience and wisdom manifest concretely through the charity system. The connection is causal: because God is omniscient, He designed charity to address specific social needs. Abstract divine attributes have concrete social policy implications.

Insight 3: The Legislative Spine 92.5

Chain: religion → prohibit → last → among → woman → cannot
[5:3] Prohibited foods (dead animals, blood, pigs, idolatrous dedications)
↓
[9:29] Fighting those who don't prohibit what God has prohibited
↓
[4:25] Marriage rules for those who cannot afford free believing women
↓
[2:282] Financial transaction documentation requirements
Dietary law, enforcement, marriage regulation, and financial transparency share a common logical backbone: the concept of divine limits ("prohibit" / "cannot") that structure all areas of human life under a single coherent legal framework.

Insight 4: Prostration ↔ Cosmic Enmity 92.5

Chain: long → prostrate → fall → though → enemy
[76:26] "During the night, fall prostrate before Him"
↓
[18:50] Angels prostrated before Adam, except Satan who became an enemy
↓
[60:1] "You shall not befriend My enemies and your enemies"
Prostration is not merely ritual — it is the defining act that separates allies from enemies of God. This cosmic division (angel vs. Satan) has direct bearing on human social relationships.

Insight 5: Life-Death ↔ Angelic Custody 92.5

Chain: ultimately → return → death → put → charge
[9:27] "GOD redeems whomever He wills. GOD is Forgiver, Most Merciful."
↓
[2:28] "You were dead, He gave you life, He puts you to death, He brings you back to life"
↓
[32:11] "You will be put to death by the angel in whose charge you are placed"
Redemption, mortality, and angelic administration are three facets of a single divine system — not independent theological topics. The transitive chain unifies them into one coherent argument.

Insight 6: Narration as Revelation 92.5

Chain: community → narrate → past → news → present
[7:101] "We narrate to you the history of those communities"
↓
[11:100] "This is news from the past communities that we narrate to you"
↓
[3:44] "This is news from the past that we reveal to you. You were not there."
The Quran frames historical narration as proof of divine communication — the Prophet could not have known these stories ("he was not there"), so their narration itself is evidence of revelation. This self-referential argument runs as a structural thread across multiple surahs.

Architecture: Infinite Loop

Build Graph
→
AutoResearch
Optimize Params
→
Rebuild Graph
↓
Infinite Deduction
→
Infinite Analysis
→
Meta-Graph
↓
Curated Insights
→
New Questions
↻
Runs indefinitely with no human intervention. Each cycle improves the graph, generates new deductions, and discovers new cross-theme connections.

Future Work

  • Embedding Optimization — Replace TF-IDF with transformer embeddings for deeper semantic similarity
  • Contradiction Detection — Identify verses that appear to conflict and surface reconciliation paths through the graph
  • Comparative Analysis — Validate deductions against classical tafsir scholarship (Ibn Kathir, al-Tabari, al-Qurtubi)
  • Multilingual Graphs — Build parallel knowledge graphs from Arabic source text
  • Interactive Explorer — Web interface for browsing deduction chains in real time

Quran Knowledge Graph — Autonomous Discovery Engine
